dataflow.name: Australian National Accounts Key Aggregates
dataflow.description: Headline statistics from the quarterly national accounts. Includes gross domestic product (GDP), GDP per capita, real income measures, and the household saving ratio

MEASURE.label: Measure
notes: Supports multi-select arrays; keep the list short and prefer aggregates before drilling down.

DATA_ITEM.label: Data Item

values.label: Gross value added per hour worked market sector
Real net national disposable income per capita
Real unit of labour costs - Non-farm

notes: Supports multi-select arrays; keep the list short and prefer aggregates before drilling down.

TSEST.label: Adjustment Type
TSEST.description: An original time series can be decomposed into three components: the trend (the general direction of the series), the seasonal component (systematic, calendar related movements) and the irregular component (unsystematic, short term fluctuations). Seasonally adjusted series are produced by estimating the seasonal component and removing this from the original series. In most economic data the seasonal component is a combination of seasonal influences (e.g. the effect of the weather or social traditions) plus other kinds of calendar related variations such as Chinese New Year and Christmas. The seasonal adjustment methodology takes into account both seasonal and other calendar related factors that evolve over time to reflect changes in activity patterns.
notes: Supports multi-select arrays; keep the list short and prefer aggregates before drilling down.
Some metadata codes currently have no published observations; requesting them may return 404.

REGION.label: Region
notes: Some metadata codes currently have no published observations; requesting them may return 404.
Includes aggregate totals (e.g., TOT/ALL) for high-level requests.

FREQ.label: Frequency
FREQ.description: Rate at which data is collated.

values.label: Quarterly
values.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every quarter.

missingCodes.label: Hourly
missingCodes.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every hour.

missingCodes.label: Daily
missingCodes.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every day.

missingCodes.label: Minutely
missingCodes.description: While N denotes "minutely", usually, there may be no observations every minute (for several series the frequency is usually "irregular" within a day/days). And though observations may be sparse (not collected or disseminated every minute), missing values do not need to be given for the minutes when no observations exist: in any case the time stamp determines when an observation is observed.

missingCodes.label: Daily or businessweek
missingCodes.description: Similar to "daily", however there are no observations for Saturdays and Sundays (so, neither "missing values" nor "numeric values" should be provided for Saturday and Sunday). This treatment ("business") is one way to deal with such cases, but it is not the only option. Such a time series could alternatively be considered daily ("D"), thus, with missing values in the weekend.

missingCodes.label: Weekly
missingCodes.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every week.

missingCodes.label: Half-yearly, semester
missingCodes.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every semester.

missingCodes.label: Annual
missingCodes.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every year.

missingCodes.label: Monthly
missingCodes.description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every month.

notes: Some metadata codes currently have no published observations; requesting them may return 404.

general: Provide `dimensionFilters` entries for every listed dimension using arrays of SDMX codes (even for single selections).
Use `startPeriod`/`endPeriod` to control the time window; `TIME_PERIOD` remains at the observation level in the resolver.
Default to aggregates (AUS, TOTAL, `_T`) before drilling into detailed geography/industry unless the planner explicitly requests depth.
Multi-select is supported—list multiple codes per dimension to compare industries/products in one request.
compatibilityHints: MEASURE controls the metric (e.g., persons, rates, dollar value). Stabilize MEASURE before adjusting other dimensions to avoid ambiguous requests.
